title: spergula marginata var. robertii
intro: Spergula marginata var. robertii, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herbaceous plant of the family Polygonaceae, with a natural distribution across Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include a high concentration of oxalic acid, a compound with astringent, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Wood Sorrel include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic, making it useful in treating conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. Wood Sorrel is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although its consumption should be limited due to its high oxalic acid content.
